source: The Market (themarket.ch) — There is no playbook; inflation, tech earnings power
summary: Record new stock issuances (IPOs/SPACs) are creating attractive entry-point opportunities
summary_extended: Baker argues that the record wave of IPOs and SPAC issuances from 2020-2021, many of which have since sold off dramatically from their peak valuations, is creating an attractive hunting ground for long-term investors to identify mispriced assets. His reasoning is that indiscriminate selling pressure — driven by the broader growth/tech multiple compression and investor disillusionment with newly public companies — has pushed many of these names well below their intrinsic value, creating asymmetric entry points. The investment implication is that patient, fundamental investors should be actively screening the post-IPO/SPAC wreckage for high-quality businesses that were caught in the downdraft rather than impaired by genuine business deterioration. Baker does not name specific companies or sectors within this cohort, keeping the thesis at the thematic level of a generalized opportunity set rather than a specific actionable position.
detail: Baker notes that the record number of new stock issues — many of which have sold off sharply from peak valuations — is generating a hunting ground for long-term investors to find mispriced assets at attractive prices.
confidence_evidence: Explicitly cited as a topic in the lede with the word 'attractive opportunities'
quote_fragment: record number of new stock issues is creating attractive opportunities
